Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to create a comprehensive event log suitable for process mining based on the provided ERP tables The log needs specific columns Case ID Activity Name Timestamp Resource Actor and Other Attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Orders Table Defines the start of the process Order Lines Table Defines picking activities Delivery Table Defines dispatch and arrival Invoice Table Defines Invoice Issued Payments Table Defines Payment Received

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since item_picking_date varies per line item treating each line as a Pick Item event is valid Given the instruction produce one event per activity if it is meaningful creating a Pick Item event per line is the most faithful representation of the data provided

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Determine Case ID Prompt instruction Use the order_id as the case identifier Decision All events will have the order_id as the Case ID

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Determine Activities and Mapping Orders to Order Created Order Lines to Item Picked Delivery to Order Dispatched and Order Arrived Invoice to Invoice Issued Payments to Payment Received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Determine Resources Actors Orders sales_person_id Order Lines picker_id Delivery carrier_id Invoice billing_person_id Payments No specific actor ID in the table

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For Payments I will leave Resource as System or Customer or leave blank Actually payments usually come from the customer I will mark Resource as Customer for payments

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Data Enrichment Joining I need to look up names from the Resources Table SP 001 Alice Brown SP 002 Bob Smith SP 003 Charlie Kim WH 001 David Lin WH 002 Eva Johnson WH 003 Frank Miller CR 01 GlobalTransport CR 02 FastShip CR 03 AirExpress BL 001 Gina Carter BL 002 Harry Lee BL 003 Irene Park

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Chronological Sorting Once all events are generated I must sort the final table by Timestamp and Case ID as secondary sort

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Handling Missing Ambiguous Data Order 1004 Has an Order and Order Lines but no Delivery Invoice or Payment data in the provided tables I must include the events that do exist I will not invent events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment Link Payments link to invoice_id I need to map invoice_id back to order_id to assign the correct Case ID INV 1001 Order 1001 INV 1002 Order 1002 INV 1003 Order 1002 INV 1004 Order 1003 INV 1005 Order 1005

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Drafting the Events Order 1001 Created Pick Dispatch Arrive Invoice Payment Order 1002 Created Pick Pick Dispatch Dispatch Arrive Arrive Invoice Invoice Payment Payment Order 1003 Created Pick Pick Dispatch Arrive Invoice Payment Order 1004 Created Pick Order 1005 Created Pick Pick Dispatch Arrive Invoice Payment

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction based on prompt Prompt suggests Order Dispatched and Order Arrived I will use these names but ensure the timestamp matches the specific delivery record

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: To stay strictly aligned with the prompts suggested activity names Order Dispatched and Order Arrived I will use those names If an order has two shipments there will be two Order Dispatched events This is acceptable in event logs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints Case ID order_id All events included Yes Chronological Yes Assumptions documented Yes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Check of Data Check Order 1004 Only Order and Line exist No Delivery Invoice Payment Check Order 1002 Two deliveries two invoices two payments Ensure all delivery and financial events are captured Check Resources Ensure IDs match the Resources table provided

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait I need to check the exact timestamps again to sort correctly Okay I need to be very careful with the sorting in the final output
